Motion To Quash Defendants Deposition Subpoenas Or In The Alternative To Modify And Or Limit Deposition Subpoenas; Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-27-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF BENJAMIN RUBIN's Motion To Quash Defendants Deposition Subpoenas Or In The Alternative To Modify And Or Limit Deposition Subpoenas; Request For Sanctions.

The report and recommendation of the judge pro tem is adopted. Plaintiff's motion for an award of sanctions is granted in the amount of $2,310.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

Motion To Quash Defendants Deposition Subpoenas Or In The Alternative To Modify And Or Limit Deposition Subpoenas; Request For Sanctions

MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Neda Hefzi, esq. (415-956-9245) of Rahman Law for Plaintiff/Moving party.
Michael J. Daley, esq. (925-225-6838) for Defendant/opposing party via Court Call.

Defendant's Counsel does not stipulate to the matter being heard by the Judge Pro Tem.
ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF BENJAMIN RUBIN's Motion To Quash Defendants Deposition Subpoenas Or In The Alternative To Modify And Or Limit Deposition Subpoenas; Request For Sanctions.

The matter is continued to December 27, 2019 at 9:30 am in Department 302, Law and Motion Department.
